methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $400M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Sage Geosystems is a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Cindy Taff and Lance Cook, currently valued at $400M post-money following $114M+ in total capital raised. The comparable universe consists of five same-sector, same-stage private companies—Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita Factory ($120M)—alongside one public peer (LNZA), providing a substantive reference set for frontier-tech valuation.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage advancement to the $400M base, implying a $760M valuation. The peer-median method yields $310M, derived from the median of the five-company peer set, which ranges from $120M to $5.0B. The wide range reflects heterogeneity in technology risk and commercialization progress within the sector.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $264M (low) to $874M (high), with a median of $535M. The current $400M valuation sits within this range but in the lower half, suggesting modest upside potential under stage-progression logic. Key uncertainties include the applicability of the Commonwealth Fusion Systems and Pano AI comparables—both substantially larger—and the sector-wide dispersion in development stage and capital efficiency. Further clarity on Sage's technical validation and customer traction would narrow the range considerably.
